books.title: Design as Art
books.author: Bruno Munari
books.motif: Saw this cute book in MoMA PS1 bookstore and I knew I have to get it. It's the first physical book I read in a while.

books.title: Stories of Your Life and Others
books.author: Ted Chiang
books.motif: It's the basis of the movie Arrival.
books.cover: shelf/book cover/Stories of Your Life and Others.png

books.title: How to Take Smart Notes
books.author: Sönke Ahrens
books.motif: Classic note-taking guide, wanna see if I can be further inspired.
books.comment: I love how it also talks about the mindset of learning and writing.
books.cover: shelf/book cover/How to Take Smart Notes.png

books.title: The Vegetarian
books.author: Han Kang
books.motif: Asia's first international Booker Prize winner in literature
books.comment: This book tells the story of a hopeless final escape. It’s about letting life return to its most innocent and stable form, so that the pain can never catch her again.​

books.title: The Borrowed
books.author: Chan Ho-Kei
books.comment: Readers will realize just how grand this story is by the time they finish it. The interlocking plot creates an overwhelming sense of a “larger” perspective, showing how everyone is just a small part of a butterfly effect hurricane. It is definitely one of the best crime novels I’ve ever read.​

books.title: The House Where I Died Once With Obi
books.author: Keigo Higashino
books.comment: Nice read. The diary format adds such a personal touch to the suspense. It makes the mystery feel more intimate, as if you’re discovering secrets right alongside the character.​
books.cover: shelf/book cover/The House Where I Died Once.png

books.title: Ark
books.author: Yuki Haruo
books.comment:
I still remember the shock of reaching the ending. The way the character switches situation from passive to aggressive is incredible. It shows how the story is truly about humanity, selfishness, and madness.

​Personally, I don’t mind a hard-to-guess thriller as long as it delivers a great story.

books.title: Klein Bottle
books.author: Kodansha Bunko
books.comment: It’s hard to believe that this book was published back in 1991. It feels like a prophetic book to me, as I think we are no longer far away from the technologies it described.

books.title: The Seventh Day
books.author: Yu Hua
books.comment: Yu Hua’s writing is remarkably fearless, weaving together so many tragedies and layers of pain. I believe I will return to this book in the future to see how my perspective and feelings toward the stories and characters evolve over time.​

books.title: Small Team Management Manual for Less than 10 People
books.author: Katsuhiko Horinouchi
books.motif: Wanna improve my management skill as a small team lead
books.comment: It was worth the time. I got several useful insights.
books.cover: shelf/book cover/Small Team Management Manual for Less than 10 People.png

books.title: Holy Mother
books.author: Akiyoshi Rikako
books.comment: It was worth the time. The mother-daughter bond is so strong that it gives them the ultimate power for revenge. Their connection becomes a weapon, proving that love can be just as fierce as it is tender.​

books.title: The World According to Garp
books.author: John Irving
books.comment: The plot keeps surprising me. But I love it. It's a long long book across several generations. I enjoyed seeing how things start and then quiet down while leaving a permanent scar; and how to stick together as the most fragile individuals...
books.cover: shelf/book cover/The World According to Garp.png

books.title: Fight Club
books.author: Chuck Palahniuk
books.motif: Classic first read among Chuck's work.
books.comment:
I actually read the book before watching the movie. I enjoyed them equally, but in slightly different ways. The book allowed me to sense the narrator's mental state more deeply, while the movie filled in the visual gaps that my imagination had left blank.

If you loved the movie, I’d highly recommend reading the book as well!

books.title: A Man Called Ove
books.author: Fredrik Backman
books.motif: Wanna know how the book would discuss the topic of death/suicide.
books.comment: Very warm book. Would recommend as a night time reading.
